text: We do not live in a world of lies. We live in a world of agreements no one has read.
Nothing is more dangerous than a man who has read nothing and believes everything.
The system does not need to convince you. It needs to exhaust you.
A metric stops describing reality the moment it starts controlling behaviour.
Order is rented. Maintenance is the invoice.
The relevant biography is scattered throughout the work. Some of it has been disguised better than the rest.
Convenience does not need permission once it becomes infrastructure.
Stability must be maintained, not assumed.
